reason: Backend development conventions and pre-development checklist for M6
M6 adds a manual library module and API endpoints while preserving layer boundaries
M6 changes storage, rebuild, API, and tests; quality rules cover double-buffer safety and regression coverage
M6 validation, upload, delete, and rebuild failures must keep structured service errors
M6 mutation logs must avoid raw text, secrets, and high-cardinality labels
M6 remains file-backed and should use atomic writes and schema discipline
M6 spans config, API, manual metadata, storage, rebuild, cache, and docs
M6 should reuse M5 ManualMetadata and existing rebuild/storage helpers
M6 builds directly on M5 manual metadata and tag-aware retrieval requirements
M5 design defines metadata contracts, sidecar loading, filters, and compatibility assumptions
